prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on an outdoor scene with a group of young people gathered around a dark car in a park-like setting. A fountain or statue is visible in the background. Several people pose casually; one person holds up a ball. Large superimposed script across the lower part of the image reads “Coke adds life,” and a red Coca-Cola crate/sign is visible near the car. The frame also contains archival watermark text.

The scene then cuts abruptly to an indoor laundromat or laundry area with orange machines in the background. A close-up shows a smiling woman wearing a blue headscarf or towel wrapped around her hair and a textured robe or coat collar. She appears to be looking toward someone off-camera or just beside her.

A wider shot reveals an older man in a light-colored suit and tie standing beside the woman. He holds up a light-colored patterned garment, inspecting or displaying it. The woman stands next to him, holding what appears to be money or a small packet in one hand. Behind them are front-loading laundry machines with round black doors and control panels above.

The commercial alternates between close-ups of the woman and medium shots of the two people at the machines. In the next close-up, the woman looks downward and slightly to the side, her expression attentive and engaged. The following wider shot shows the man now holding or examining white laundry items, while the woman holds or looks at a patterned piece of fabric near him. Their body language suggests they are discussing or evaluating the laundry, but no spoken content is visible.

Another close-up shows the woman looking down with a calmer, more thoughtful expression. The orange laundromat machines remain blurred in the background.

The final frame is a product end card: a large “CLOROX” logo in white block letters inside a dark diamond shape with a red border, set against a blue background. Small text appears near the bottom, including “1975 The Clorox Company,” though some of the surrounding fine print is difficult to read clearly.
